perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall pay, or cause to be paid, the Fees shown on an invoice no later than the last business day of the month Recipient received such invoice unless disputed in accordance with Section 5.7. Any amount not received from the invoiced Party within such period shall bear interest at the Applicable Rate, from and including the last date of such period to, but excluding, the date of payment.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall pay, or cause to be paid, the Fees shown on an invoice no later than 45 days of the month Recipient received such invoice unless disputed in accordance with Section 5.7. Any amount not received from the invoiced Party within such period shall bear interest at the Applicable Rate, from and including the last date of such period to, but excluding, the date of payment.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding payment terms. Initially, Section 5.4 states payment should be made by the last business day of the month of receipt, but it is modified to 45 days later. This contradicts with Section 6.3 (a) that allows termination if a material breach occurs if not cured within thirty (30) days of written notice thereof, because it's not clear if the invoice must be paid in 30 days (cure period for breach) or 45 days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate when the Termination Date has occurred for all Services. In addition, this Agreement may be terminated by either Party (the "Terminating Party") upon written notice to the other Party (which notice, in case of material breach, shall specify the basis for such claim for breach), if: (a) the other Party or its Affiliates materially breaches this Agreement and such breach is not cured, to the reasonable satisfaction of the Terminating Party, within thirty (30) days of written notice thereof, it being understood that a good-faith dispute over an invoice or Service shall not constitute a material breach of this Agreement; or (b) the other Party files for bankruptcy or similar proceeding, is the subject of an involuntary filing for bankruptcy or similar proceeding (not dismissed within sixty (60) days), makes a general assignment of all or substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, becomes or is declared insolvent, becomes the subject of any proceedings (not dismissed within sixty (60) days) related to its liquidation, insolvency, bankruptcy or the appointment of a trustee or a receiver, takes any corporate action for its winding up or dissolution, or a court approves reorganization proceedings on such Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment terms from 'the last business day of the month' to '45 days of the month'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which allows for termination if a material breach (like non-payment) is not cured within 30 days of written notice. It's unclear whether payment must be made within 30 or 45 days, creating uncertainty and a potential basis for wrongful termination if the party terminates after 30 days but before 45.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to each of the Services, the term thereof will be for a period commencing as of the date hereof, unless a different date is specified as the commencement date for any applicable Service on Exhibit A or Exhibit B (either, a "Commencement Date"), and shall continue until 12 months following the Commencement Date unless (i) such other date as is specified as the termination date for any applicable Service in this Agreement or on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, as applicable (the "Term") or (ii) earlier terminated pursuant to this Agreement (a "Termination Date").
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to each of the Services, the term thereof will be for a period commencing as of the date hereof, unless a different date is specified as the commencement date for any applicable Service on Exhibit A or Exhibit B (either, a "Commencement Date"), and shall continue until 12 months following the Commencement Date unless (i) such other date as is specified as the termination date for any applicable Service in this Agreement or on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, as applicable (the "Term") or (ii) earlier terminated pursuant to this Agreement (a "Termination Date"), or (iii) unless extended by mutual agreement of the parties, up to a maximum of 36 months.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds an in-text contradiction by allowing the service term to be extended by mutual agreement, up to 36 months. However, Exhibit A and B, specifically sections G1.1, G1.2 and G1.3 state that the termination can only occur as per an agreed Migration Plan. This now creates an in-text contradiction because there could be a case where an extension goes beyond the agreed migration plan, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 6.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A Transition Services Section G1: IT Services12 Service Name Description of Service Term Monthly Fee (USD) - Commencement Date - 2020 Monthly Fee (USD) - 2021 G1.1 IT Service Category: Major Applications - Hosting and Infrastructure Support Hosting - shared and dedicated environments Provision of infrastructure and hosting services at RGHI's data center for shared hardware and hardware dedicated to RCP's systems. Services include: • Access to and use of the noted applications groups • Disaster Recovery • Administration • Security management • Help Desk services • Backup/restore management Service also includes provision of equivalent access to this set of RCP applications in alternative data center(s) upon migration to RCP's new operating environment(s), and/or equivalent services from alternative providers, managed under this Agreement by RGHI. All services in group 24 months from the Commencement Date Termination can only be as per an agreed Migration Plan G1.1.1 Autosys Job Scheduling and Monitoring System. $10,109 TBD 1 Where reference is made to RGHI's data center, this means either (1) the Lincolnshire facility at 605 Heathrow Drive, (2) the Lake Forest backup data center at 1900 West Field Court, or (3) the Cloud Service provider selected to house certain infrastructure operations from time to time during the Term and migration. 2 Fees for 2021 are not yet finalized, and will be negotiated in good faith by RGHI and RCP during the course of 2020.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the term's duration with specific termination dates. The changed text introduces an extension clause allowing up to 36 months, directly contradicting Exhibit A's Section G1, which states, 'Termination can only be as per an agreed Migration Plan.' The extension could potentially exceed the Migration Plan's timeframe, creating an inconsistency in how and when the services can be terminated. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', specifically when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: The indemnities provided for in Section 9.1 shall be the sole and exclusive monetary remedy of the Parties hereto and their Affiliates and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, representatives, successors and permitted assigns for any breach of or inaccuracy in any representation or warranty or any breach, nonfulfillment or default in the performance of any of the covenants or agreements contained in this Agreement, and the Parties shall not be entitled to a rescission of this Agreement or to any further indemnification rights or claims of any nature whatsoever in respect thereof (including any common law rights of contribution), all of which the Parties hereto hereby waive.
perturbation.changed_text: The indemnities provided for in Section 9.1 shall be the sole and exclusive monetary remedy of the Parties hereto and their Affiliates and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, representatives, successors and permitted assigns for any breach of or inaccuracy in any representation or warranty or any breach, nonfulfillment or default in the performance of any of the covenants or agreements contained in this Agreement, except for cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, and the Parties shall not be entitled to a rescission of this Agreement or to any further indemnification rights or claims of any nature whatsoever in respect thereof (including any common law rights of contribution), all of which the Parties hereto hereby waive.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an exception for gross negligence or willful misconduct to the exclusive remedy provision. This contradicts Section 10.3, which states that the Parties shall be entitled to seek an injunction to prevent actual or threatened breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, this being in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. Because of the added expection in Section 9.2, now it's not clear if parties are able to enforce specific performance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall be entitled to seek an injunction to prevent actual or threatened breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, this being in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing contained herein shall prevent a Party from seeking damages (to the extent permitted herein) in the event that specific performance is not available.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an exclusive remedy in Section 9.1, which waives all other rights. The changed text adds an exception for 'gross negligence or willful misconduct.' The contradicted text, specifically in Section 10.3, entitles parties to seek injunctive relief and specific performance, 'in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity.' By creating an exception to the exclusive remedy provision, it now becomes unclear whether parties can still pursue injunctive relief or specific performance in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, contradicting the original intention of Section 10.3. This creates an inconsistency regarding the available remedies, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'
